div.h1: Compiler Output: AoS vs SoA Assembly Analysis
div.h2: Implementation Guide: Vectorization & Cache Locality Optimization

g.title: Stride-32 byte access patterns cause cache fragmentation

div.p: The AoS approach forces the CPU to perform 16 separate memory requests, pulling 16 full cache lines (1024 bytes) into L1 just to extract 64 bytes of relevant data. The SoA approach utilizes the full width of the AVX-256 registers, loading only 2 cache lines to process 8 particles in parallel using a single FMA (Fused Multiply-Add) operation.

g.text: Struct Particle { float x, y, z, _p, vx, vy, vz, _p2; } // 32 Bytes
Struct Particles { float x[N], vx[N], ... } // Contiguous
vfmadd213ps ymm0, ymm1, ymm2 ; Parallel FMA

g.title: Stride-32 byte access patterns cause cache fragmentation
